task: Fixed Claude PID tracking and session lifecycle management across three files to prevent accidental context compaction during multi-session workflows
discussion_summary: Added pgrep+lsof fallback PID detection in session_start.py and pre_compact.py, implemented proper return bool signatures in lifecycle.py kill functions, and added cooldown control to daemon loop. User raised critical concern: current implementation kills the FIRST PID found, but they routinely run 2+ simultaneous Claude Code sessions in the same repo — killing the wrong session is a real risk. This needs architectural rethinking before the fix is production-safe.

accomplishments.what: Added _write_claude_pid() with pgrep+lsof fallback to session_start.py
Fixed _wait_for_exchange_end_then_kill() to return bool and use PID fallback in lifecycle.py
Updated daemon loop to use kill return value for cooldown control
Added pgrep fallback to pre_compact.py PID lookup
Verified syntax validity and constant presence across all three files

next_actions.action: CRITICAL: Redesign PID tracking to support multi-session workflows. Current approach kills first matching PID — unsafe when 2+ Claude sessions run in same repo. Options: (a) write session-specific PID file with UUID suffix, (b) track PID in memory-mapped state with session context, (c) use process group IDs instead of single PID. Evaluate which fits askr's architecture.
next_actions.why: User explicitly flagged this as a blocker — current fix breaks their actual workflow. Must resolve before this code is safe to use.

next_actions.action: Once multi-session strategy is chosen, update _write_claude_pid() and pre_compact.py lookup logic to respect session isolation. Add integration test with 2 simultaneous sessions to verify correct PID is killed.
next_actions.why: Prevents regression and validates the fix works for real-world usage patterns.

next_actions.action: Commit the three files (session_start.py, lifecycle.py, pre_compact.py) with message 'fix(session-lifecycle): add PID tracking with pgrep fallback — multi-session support pending'
next_actions.why: Code is syntactically valid and improves single-session reliability. Multi-session fix can land in follow-up commit once strategy is chosen.

next_actions.action: Document the multi-session limitation in a blocker or decision note so future sessions don't re-solve this without addressing the architectural gap.
next_actions.why: Prevents silent failures and makes the constraint explicit for the next developer.

decisions.decision: Implemented pgrep+lsof fallback for PID detection instead of relying solely on Claude's process tree
decisions.reason: Increases robustness across different Claude launch methods (CLI, app, etc.)

decisions.decision: Added return bool to kill functions to enable cooldown control in daemon loop
decisions.reason: Allows graceful backoff when kill succeeds, preventing tight retry loops

user_rejected_decisions.what_was_proposed: Current PID-killing approach as production-ready for multi-session workflows
user_rejected_decisions.user_signal: killing the first pid doesn't really work, cause I usually run around 2 simultaneous claude code sessions in a singular repo

relational_files.why: Core daemon loop that uses kill return value for cooldown control
Calls PID lookup logic to prevent accidental compaction during active sessions
Writes the PID that lifecycle and pre_compact depend on

blockers: Multi-session PID isolation: current implementation kills first matching PID, unsafe when 2+ Claude sessions run in same repo. Needs architectural redesign before production use.
